content.full_text:
Ken Finney  
Ted Prim  
Section 84206 Terminating Committee

John Corzine of the Legislative Counsel asked whether a controlled committee of a retiring officeholder could file its semi-annual statement early. He indicated to me that the committee did not intend to engage in any political activity after it filed its statement.

Even though the committee is not covered by 18426, you and I decided to permit the committee to file early since the commission has ratified previous staff advice which permitted certain persons to file semi-annual statements early. We agreed that 18426 should be amended to include the control committee of the retiring officeholder.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a controlled committee of a retiring officeholder could file its semi-annual statement early when the committee does not intend to engage in political activity after filing.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC permitted the committee to file its semi-annual statement early based on prior commission ratification of similar early filing permissions, and agreed that Section 18426 should be amended to explicitly include retiring officeholders' controlled committees.

He indicated to me that the committee did not intend to engage in any political activity after it filed its statement. Even though the committee is not covered by 18426, you and I decided to permit the committee to file early since the commission has ratified previous staff advice which permitted certain persons to file semi-annual statements early. embedding.summary: FPPC staff advised that a retiring officeholder's controlled committee may file its semi-annual statement early when ceasing political activity, and recommended amending Section 18426 to codify this practice.
